title: ranunculus aquatilis subsp. radians
intro: Ranunculus aquatilis subsp. radians, commonly known as the creeping buttercup, is a subspecies of aquatic plant that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. The creeping buttercup can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote wound healing and reduce the risk of infection.
